publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-06-29 18:03:14 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

origination.label: creator
origination.persname: Edelman, Hendrik. (Title of work: Dutch-American bibliography 1693-1794.)

did.abstract: Alphbetical files of Assistant Director Hendrik Edelman concerning collection development.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Hendrik Edelman served as Assistant Director of the Cornell University Libraries from 1969 to 1979, and then became University Librarian at Rutgers University.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Hendrik Edelman papers, #13-1-1887. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Includes alphabetical files of Assistant Director Hendrik Edelman as the Assistant Director of the Cornell University Libraries concerning collection development, 1969-1974.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Also, files relating to research on his book, Dutch-American Bibliography 1693-1794, a descriptive catalog of Dutch-language books, pamphlets, and almanacs printed in America, 1974; research, consulting, teaching, and other professional papers relating to immigrant presses in America and to library collection development; videos of a Pennsylvania State Library teleconference on collection development; and publications by Edelman.

controlaccess.subject: Librarians -- United States.
Dutch imprints -- United States -- Publishing -- History.
Libraries and publishing -- United States.
Academic libraries -- Collection development.
Collection development (Libraries).

did.unittitle: Reprints Committee, Center for Urban Development

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes notes, reprints, copies and "A Guide to the Archives of the Netherlands Museum,Holland, Michigan, 1978"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes notes, reprints, copies and Collection Development Tapes from the Suffolk Community College

did.unittitle: Dutch-American bibliography and note cards, microfilm, 1976 CLR file
Research, consulting, professional, immigrant presses in America
Preliminary Marketing Report for Research Publications Inc
List of materials on The Detschnationale Volkspartei
The Collections of the Historical Society of Pennsylvania
Report: A Study on Materials Availability, Adelphi University
Discussion on German Periodicals, by Charles Brown

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Logos, Library Journal, the Book Publishing Annual, Christian Book Publishing and Distribution and publications on eighteenth century English books, library research, computing etc. Also includes articles and interviews by Edelman

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes books on library planning, library search tools, library collection management, university libraries, library journals, computer innovation, obituaries etc.

did.unittitle: "Selection Methodology in Academic Libraries", paper presented at ALA preconference
Report on the First Year's Activities Under the Cornell Mellon Project
Other immigrant publishers of note - in America
Navigeren od de Stroomversnellingen van de Tijd
Concept von een kroniek van de Elsevier Publishing Company
